ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for plain pancake; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared grain-based snack foods is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium plain pancake weighing approximately 77 g, served without toppings.

nutrient_source.title: Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe
nutrient_source.organization: Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) — Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at 0.95 confidence for all per_100g fields. Core macros and micronutrients are taken directly from the HPB Foodo entry for plain pancakes. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18263 (Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat; the USDA value of 1.9 g/100g is used as a close analogue and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: Pancakes, made from packet mix — GI value 67 (Atkinson et al., 2008)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 67 for plain pancakes is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC, Diabetes Care 2008). The closest listed entry is 'Pancakes, made from packet mix' with GI 67 (glucose reference). This is used as an analogous estimate for plain homemade pancakes given the similar refined wheat flour base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 × 23.6) / 100 ≈ 15.8, rounded to 15.9 using 77 g serving size (30.7 g carb/100g × 0.77 = 23.6 g carb per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 92 mg/100g — well below the 600 mg/100g high-sodium threshold for solid foods
hypertension.guidance: Plain pancakes are low in sodium (92 mg/100g) and are generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension when consumed without high-sodium toppings such as butter, processed cheese, or savoury sauces; however, adding syrup or sweet toppings may indirectly worsen cardiometabolic risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI of 67 combined with low dietary fibre (1.9 g/100g) and refined wheat flour base produces significant postprandial glycaemic excursion at typical serving sizes
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit plain pancakes due to the medium GI (67), low fibre content (1.9 g/100g), and a glycaemic load of approximately 15.9 per serving; substituting wholemeal or oat flour, reducing portion size to one small pancake, and pairing with protein (e.g., eggs or Greek yogurt) can help attenuate the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate base with low fibre (1.9 g/100g) and moderate fat (7.8 g/100g including saturated fat ~1.9 g/100g) contributes to atherogenic dietary patterns when consumed regularly
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain pancakes made from refined wheat flour provide low dietary fibre and moderate saturated fat, which may contribute to unfavourable lipid profiles and elevated cardiovascular risk when consumed frequently; individuals with cardiovascular disease should prefer wholegrain alternatives, limit portion size, and avoid high-fat or high-sugar toppings.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined wheat flour base with minimal fibre (1.9 g/100g) and no meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients (omega-3, polyphenols, or antioxidants)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Plain pancakes offer no anti-inflammatory benefit for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to their refined carbohydrate base and negligible fibre; substituting wholemeal flour and adding anti-inflammatory toppings such as berries or ground flaxseed is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Plain pancakes are flat, round, pan-fried or griddle-cooked cakes made from a simple batter of refined wheat flour, eggs, milk, butter, baking powder, and salt. They are cooked on a lightly greased hot griddle or frying pan until golden-brown on both sides, producing a soft, slightly spongy interior with a lightly crisp exterior. Plain pancakes are consumed as a snack or breakfast item across many cultures and are commonly served with sweet toppings such as maple syrup, honey, or fresh fruit, though the plain preparation itself contains no added toppings. Per 100 g, plain pancakes provide 228 kcal, 7.9 g protein, 7.8 g fat, 30.7 g carbohydrate, 4.7 g sugar, 1.9 g dietary fibre, 92 mg sodium, 99 mg calcium, and 2.7 mg iron. The medium glycaemic index of 67 and low fibre content make them a moderate-to-high glycaemic concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The relatively low sodium content (92 mg/100g) makes them acceptable for hypertension management when consumed plain. The refined wheat flour base provides limited nutritional density and no meaningful anti-inflammatory compounds, making wholegrain substitutions preferable for individuals with chronic inflammatory or cardiometabolic conditions. Pancakes are classified as a processed grain-based snack food and contain gluten, eggs, and dairy allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a plain pancake?
What is the GI of pancakes?
Can I eat pancakes if I have heart disease?

confidence_reason: Core macros and micronutrients are sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database at 0.95 confidence for all fields. Saturated fat is estimated from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for plain pancakes (USDA #18263), which is an approved secondary source. GI is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. (2008) as an analogous estimate for the same food category. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These fields are not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for plain pancakes. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (entry 18263) was consulted for saturated fat (1.9 g/100g adopted) but does not resolve all micronutrient gaps for this specific preparation. Saturated fat value of 1.9 g/100g is adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry 18263 (Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat; this is documented as an adapted value. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008) for pancakes made from packet mix, applied to plain pancakes given the comparable refined wheat flour base. Glycaemic load per serving calculated using 77 g serving size: GL = (67 × 23.6) / 100 ≈ 15.9. Plain pancakes are a Western/international food with no mandatory local-language naming requirements under Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules; local_names fields are set to null accordingly. No SNOMED CT code identified for plain pancake as a prepared food item.
